_meta.note: Queries authored BEFORE the USDA slice was downloaded or inspected. Each entry captures a food-string an upstream narration parser would plausibly extract from natural meal narration. expected_intent is in plain English and is the ground truth for manual top-1 scoring; scoring does NOT reference USDA fdc_ids.

queries.expected_intent: chicken eggs prepared by scrambling; any generic egg entry with 'scrambled' preparation or plain 'eggs' is acceptable
rolled oats / porridge; generic oatmeal entry is acceptable
plain greek yogurt (nonfat/low-fat/whole — any); NOT a flavored variant unless it's the only match
user said the brand; fallback to generic greek yogurt is acceptable since our slice won't carry brands
skinless boneless chicken breast (raw or cooked — either)
chicken, any cut, cooked by grilling — breast preferred if both available
cooked white rice — portion should not confuse the matcher
cooked brown rice; long-grain is the most common variant

queries.query: two slices of whole wheat toast
queries.expected_intent: whole wheat bread (toasted or not — slice count should not confuse match)
this is two foods: bread + peanut butter. Top-1 match on either peanut butter OR bread is a partial hit; neither is a miss
raw banana
raw apple, any variety
whey protein powder or generic 'protein supplement'; USDA may not carry branded protein powders — a whey / casein entry is acceptable
two foods: coffee + milk. Either match is a partial hit
raw spinach is the dominant ingredient; top-1 on spinach is a match. Pure 'salad' with no ingredient info would be a miss
atlantic salmon, cooked — raw is acceptable
three foods: chicken + tortilla/wrap + cucumber. Partial hit on any of the three components
raw almonds; 'some' is a quantity filler and should not affect match
avocado + bread. Partial hit on either
raw or cooked broccoli
